message:
fix: exclude deferred issues from --overdue listing, preserve lone asterisks in markdown

Two unrelated bug fixes:

**list --overdue filter (list.rs):**
- When --overdue is set without an explicit --status filter, deferred
  issues were still included because the fallback `args.status.is_empty()`
  was triggering `include_deferred = true`.
- Fix: add `!args.overdue` guard so deferred issues are excluded when
  --overdue is active (unless --deferred is explicitly passed).

**Markdown asterisk stripping (markdown.rs):**
- A left-flanking single asterisk (preceded by space, followed by
  non-space) was being stripped even when there was no matching closing
  asterisk, corrupting text like "pointer *p" into "pointer p".
- Fix: when a left-flanking * is detected, scan ahead for a matching
  right-flanking * (preceded by non-space). If none is found, treat
  the asterisk as literal text and leave it in place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -351,7 +351,7 @@ fn apply_client_filters(
     let notes_needle = args.notes_contains.as_deref().map(str::to_lowercase);
     // Deferred issues are included by default when no status filter is specified
     let include_deferred = args.deferred
-        || args.status.is_empty()
+        || (!args.overdue && args.status.is_empty())
         || args
             .status
             .iter()
